reasons: Diggie can reduce the value of Chou's pickoff by protecting nearby allies during the crowd-control window.
Chou becomes less valuable if his kick does not convert into a kill.

proof.summary: Chou wants to isolate one target with crowd control, while Diggie can protect teammates with debuff removal, shield, and control immunity during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Chou's real engage.
Diggie stays near the target Chou wants to kick.
failureCases: Chou isolates Diggie before the teamfight starts.

reasons: Khufra can punish Chou's dash-based approach and make it harder for him to reach the backline.
Chou must spend more time baiting before he can safely engage.

proof.summary: Chou relies on mobility to enter and reposition, while Khufra punishes blink movement and protects the carry's space.
worksBestWhen: Khufra predicts Chou's dash or engage path.
Khufra stays near the carry Chou wants to kick.
failureCases: Chou uses control immunity timing to avoid the key punish.

reasons: Minsitthar's field limits directional blink skills, making Chou's entry and escape less flexible.
This is strong around objectives or carries because Chou wants dash access to one priority target.

proof.summary: Chou wants to dash in and out around one target, while Minsitthar can create a zone that restricts directional blink movement.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar places the field around the carry or objective.
Chou is forced to fight inside the zone.
failureCases: Minsitthar wastes ultimate before Chou commits.

reasons: Valir can push Chou away and slow his approach before Chou reaches displacement range.
Valir's self-protection makes Chou's solo pickoff harder when Valir keeps spacing.

proof.summary: Chou wants close-range displacement, while Valir can deny the approach with knockback, slow, and self-cleanse-style timing.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds knockback until Chou commits.
Valir positions behind allies and does not stand alone.
failureCases: Chou flanks from fog and catches Valir first.

reasons: Kaja can punish Chou after he uses his mobility or commits too deep for a kick.
Suppression is useful because Chou normally relies on mobility and timing to escape after displacement.

proof.summary: Chou wants to control the fight timing, while Kaja can lock him during a bad engage or after his control-immunity timing is unavailable.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits until Chou's control immunity dash is unavailable.
failureCases: Chou baits Kaja ultimate with timing.
Kaja is too far away to punish Chou's kick.
